agents.body: Absorb owner corrections and convert them into architecture rules, tests, and receipts.
agents.title: Architecture / Constitution Loop

agents.body: Maintain Agent Terrarium as a live playable working-space viewport into AI-OS, not a decorative game-about-AI-OS or dashboard skin.
agents.title: Working-Space Viewport Loop

agents.body: Maintain the whole-game plan and convert owner corrections, red-team failures, and definition-of-good changes into the next concrete game-building events.
agents.title: Full Game Plan Director Loop

agents.body: Turn AI-OS ground truth sources into game world state: organs, agents, quests, resources, fog, gates, receipts.
agents.title: Source → world.json Compiler Loop

agents.body: Evolve and enforce the living rubric for when Agent Terrarium is actually good, without lowering standards to match the current implementation.
agents.title: Definition Of Good Oracle Loop

agents.body: Render world state as an actual 2D pixel-art working-space viewport: tilemap, sprites, camera, player verbs, animation from state.
agents.title: world.json → Pixel Game Runtime Loop

agents.body: Playtest as the owner before the owner sees the artifact: attack whether it feels like a viewport into the working space, plus game feel, proof wiring, mobile reality, dashboard slop, autonomy, and hard gates.
agents.title: Owner-Proxy Red Team / Playtest Loop

agents.body: React to tests, screenshots, layout probes, critic findings, and proof failures; repair the game until receipts support claims.
agents.title: Proof / Playtest Loop

agents.body: Choose the next best event/action from current state, rubric failures, proof results, and hard-gate boundaries.
agents.title: Optimal Action Policy Loop

agents.body: Track true hard gates and only surface owner decisions when a gate is real: public deploy, spend, secrets, sends, deletes, persistent services.
agents.title: Owner Gate Router Loop

fishbowl.truth_boundary: Local read-only snapshot; no work, gate, spend, send, deploy, or credential mutation from this game slice.

fog.reason: Live world compiled, but owner-proxy red-team and screenshot proof have not accepted this slice yet.

meta.title: Agent Terrarium — Cozy AI-OS Fishbowl

organs.body: The sorting shed holds 426 captured signals until review turns them into safe work.
organs.title: Refinery Sorting Shed

organs.body: The hearth shows whether the homestead is calm, watchful, or needs attention; proof remains one tap away.
organs.title: Hearth House

organs.body: Crates are work waiting to be consumed; the source workflow has 3 open items.
organs.title: Work Granary

organs.body: Source-backed loops turn the waterwheel: visible motion means a named worker has real work and a receipt trail.
organs.title: Waterwheel Workshop

organs.body: The lookout keeps the whole working homestead visible as a place, not a disguised control panel.
organs.title: Lookout Library

organs.body: The stewardship charter keeps proof, owner gates, and no-fake-liveness law visible as fences and locked doors.
organs.title: Steward's Hall

organs.body: The latest workspace_viewport_requested event flows downstream to the workers that know how to use it.
organs.title: Mill Stream

organs.body: Tools at hand are visible without making the owner memorize machinery: enabled=21, paused=6.
organs.title: Tool Shed

organs.body: Fruit appears only when competence, consumption, taste, or token-value evidence becomes source-backed; proxy fruit stays visibly unripe.
organs.title: Proof Orchard

requirements.deterministic_inputs: source paths + mtimes + hashes + event payload

loop_architecture.contract: problem-path-specific loops over one shared event bus with per-loop receipt trails and downstream event suggestions
loop_architecture.invalid_driver_shape: slow scheduled work loop or timer-driven worker as the Terrarium work engine
loop_architecture.name: event-driven loops

objects.title: red-team-pending-fog

objects.body: Meet the Keeper for a short field lesson, then return to the world with one clear quest.

approve: Credentials, API keys, and account sign-in.
Spend, live trades, or paid generation beyond an agreed cap.
External send, publish, deploy, push or merge, and public tunnels.
Deletion and permanent theme or canon changes.
Curated vault writes outside Inbox, Logs, or Social Archives.
first_30_seconds: Move through the working room with the floating pad or WASD.
Meet the Terrarium Keeper at the guidepost, or press G from anywhere.
Follow one worker, gather one proof light, then inspect a locked gate.
Approve only when a real hard gate appears with one clear approval line.
ignore: Process IDs, loop heartbeats, and silent cron noise.
Green technical PASS without a phone-openable artifact or screenshot.
Raw vault paths as the primary handoff when you are on phone.
Requests to run scripts the crew can safely run itself.
outcomes: The quest banner names the current objective; the game message names the last consequence.
Receipt lights raise proof. A model claiming PASS does not.
A followed worker mirrors a real run; a gate check never unlocks a hard gate.
Fog means evidence is missing. Gather proof before trying to clear it.
If the crew becomes invisible, ask for the owner cockpit before more loop work.
say: Plain intent: tell the crew what outcome you want — not which commands to run.
Ask for relief in ordinary language: show proof, open the link, or tell me what needs me.
Correct the world directly: this is not a game yet, I have no link, or this still feels like a dashboard.
Choose the destination; the crew chooses the safe route and returns with evidence.

objects.title: Terrarium Keeper

viewport.success_oracle: A red-team reviewer can identify live workspace state visible and inspectable with source path/timestamp/proof.
